commit.message:
fix: add explicit @Inject() to EventsModule services for tsx compatibility

tsx (esbuild) does not support emitDecoratorMetadata, so NestJS cannot
resolve constructor parameters by type alone. Services using explicit
@Inject(TOKEN) worked, but EventsModule services relying on implicit
type-based resolution for ModuleRef received undefined, crashing
QueueWorkerEventsService.onModuleInit on startup.

Add @Inject() to all constructor parameters in:
- QueueWorkerEventsService (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- DomainEventBroadcasterAdapter (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- EventBroadcasterService (EventsGateway)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
QUBO improvements, adding circuit validation + IO, bugfixes and more (#50)

* Initial plan

* moving plotroutines into module

* Plot of parameter development wrt depths and indices, and of hit ratios

* allow_infeasible parameter in exact cover, with plotting utility

* plt instead of pl for matplotlib.pyplot

* Trying to validate portopt circuit

* Validation of quantum circuit

* Fixed circuit for generic QUBO problem, incl validation

* Moving validation of circuit from QAOA to BaseProblem

* Extending validation of QUBO problem to also check that the original cost function is the same as the transformed QUBO cost function

* Fixing exact cover circuit by inheriting QUBO

* plotting hit probabilities

* simplifying QUBO mapping and brute-force solve

* Clean-up validation of portofolio optimization

* Scaling and hamming weight as options for exact cover

* Saving optimization time per level

* hamming weight as member var

* json io interface for QAOA results

* brute force solve for exact cover

* Creating problem instances from the IO objects

* Scaling cost to [0, 1] instead of [0, 2pi]

* Fix after changing Grover mixer API (see commit aa961b6)

* incompatible libs for current version

* unit test for circuit validation

* New example for exact cover showing IO functionality + IO unittest

* Cherry-pick 24 neqst-wp1-kb commits + fix Qiskit 2.x compatibility and merge issues

Co-authored-by: havahol <5363644+havahol@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix spelling typos and error message formatting in cherry-picked code

Co-authored-by: havahol <5363644+havahol@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review comments: restore setup.py, fix Grover N_qubits, remove cost_nonQUBO from PortfolioOptimization

Co-authored-by: havahol <5363644+havahol@users.noreply.github.com>

* Delete examples/ExactCover/data/exact_cover_path_problem_xy_result.json

File should not be commited

* Replace parameter_binds with assign_parameters for Qiskit 2.x compatibility

The QAOA optimization was producing poor or inconsistent results with the
Grover mixer on Qiskit 2.3.0+ (e.g., near-random sampling instead of
proper optimization). The XY mixer worked fine. Root cause: the legacy
backend.run(parameter_binds=[...]) API has known bugs in Qiskit-Aer and
is deprecated in Qiskit 2.x. Parameterized gates like mcphase (used by
the Grover mixer) were not always bound correctly via parameter_binds,
causing the mixer to behave like an identity operation.

Fix: Bind parameters before execution using assign_parameters() instead
of passing parameter_binds to backend.run(). This is the recommended
approach in Qiskit 2.x and avoids the Aer parameter_binds code path.

Changes:
- loss(): use assign_parameters() before backend.run()
- sample_cost_landscape() sequential mode: same pattern
- sample_cost_landscape() batch mode: build list of pre-bound circuits
  instead of passing parameter lists to parameter_binds
- hist(): use assign_parameters() before backend.run()
- _eval_cost(): use assign_parameters() before backend.run()

All getParametersToBind() calls now use asList=False since assign_parameters
expects scalar values, not lists.

Updated test_qiskit2_compatibility.py comment to reflect the new approach.

* util fixes. reran notebooks

---------

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786) (#1492)

* fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786)

When go-to-definition is used on a type that lives in a different
assembly from the one FCS reports (e.g. System.Boolean forwarded from
netstandard.dll to System.Runtime.dll), ILSpy's CSharpDecompiler
throws:
  'Decompiling types that are not part of the main module is not supported'

This happens because ILSpy's type system resolves type forwarders
transparently — GetTypeDefinition returns the actual type definition
from the target assembly's module, but the decompiler was created for
the source assembly. The mismatch causes the exception.

Fix: after resolving the type definition, check whether its
ParentModule matches the decompiler's main module. If not (a type
forwarder), retrieve the actual assembly path from
IModule.PEFile.FileName and create a fresh CSharpDecompiler for
that assembly. Resolve the type definition again in the new type
system, then decompile as normal.

If the PEFile path is unavailable or the type cannot be resolved in
the redirected assembly, the original decompiler and type definition
are used unchanged — the existing try/catch will handle any remaining
failure gracefully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: skip unreadable files in rename; remove duplicate log in textDocument/implementation (#1491)

* fix: skip unreadable files in rename and log warnings in textDocument/implementation

Fixes two code quality issues in AdaptiveFSharpLspServer.fs:

1. TextDocumentRename: skip files that cannot be read
   When building workspace edits for rename, if a file cannot be opened
   (e.g., SourceLinked virtual paths like /_/src/Server.fs from external
   packages that FCS references but don't exist locally), return None and
   filter it out rather than generating edits for an unreadable file.
   This prevents client errors when the LSP client tries to apply edits
   to files that don't exist. See issue #1011.

2. TextDocumentImplementation: remove duplicate log + log warning on failure
   - Remove the duplicate 'TextDocumentImplementation Request: {params}'
     log that was emitted twice in the handler.
   - Log a warning when getProjectOptions fails instead of silently calling
     failwith, making these failures visible in server logs.

Now sends `null` for text summarization (omits constraint, lets backend
pick a default) and `50` for file uploads (Form field requires a value).

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

* blue example

* Apply backdrop intensity and transparency

macOS: apply visuals.backdrop_intensity to the NSVisualEffectView alpha (clamped) so backdrop intensity affects the effect view.

Windows: honor visuals.backdrop_intensity by computing an alpha-packed accent color, toggle WS_EX_LAYERED for transparent windows, call SetLayeredWindowAttributes and DwmExtendFrameIntoClientArea when needed, and set the AccentPolicy accordingly (enable blur for transparent or non-none backdrops, set gradient_color based on intensity, and set accent_flags depending on DWM attribute result).

Tests: update expected backdrop_intensity in a unit test from 4.0/1.0 to 0.25 and assert a platform op is emitted. These changes ensure backdrop intensity and transparency settings are respected across platforms.

Addresses review comment: consumers should not need to specify the
string format. updateState now detects JSON (starts with '{') and
treats everything else as YAML, converting via SnakeYAML before
passing to the WASM boundary.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>

* feat: move YAML conversion into WASM module

serde_yaml 0.9 compiles to wasm32-unknown-unknown, so YAML conversion
can be owned by the WASM module rather than each language binding.

- Move serde_yaml from cfg-gated to regular [dependencies]
- Remove #[cfg(not(target_family = "wasm"))] gate from yaml module
- Remove cfg gate from FlagEvaluator::update_state_from_yaml
- Expose update_state_from_yaml as a WASM export
- update_state auto-detects JSON vs YAML ('{' prefix = JSON, else YAML)

- updateInstance now throws EvaluatorException on success=false

Bindings are now format-agnostic: pass JSON or YAML, WASM handles both.

- Python: update_state accepts str (JSON or YAML), remove update_state_from_yaml
- Update all Python tests to pass JSON strings instead of dicts
- serde_yaml promoted to regular dependency (no cfg gates)
- Add use pyo3::types::PyDict (not re-exported by pyo3::prelude in 0.22)
- Serialize WASM unit tests with static mutex to prevent global-state races

* refactor: remove updateStateFromYaml convenience method

The WASM module owns YAML/JSON detection via update_state. There is no
need for a separate convenience method — updateState(String) accepts
both formats transparently. Remove updateStateFromYaml from Java and
update tests to call updateState directly. Clean up stale comment in
Python bindings.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: handle new CompiledNode variants from datalogic-rs 4.0.18

datalogic-rs 4.0.18 added CompiledVar, CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex
and CompiledStringConcat variants to CompiledNode. The walk_node_for_vars
match was non-exhaustive. Add a wildcard arm that conservatively returns
false (send full context) for any unknown variants.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(evaluator): handle CompiledVar/CompiledExists nodes and fix getrandom for WASM

datalogic-rs 4.0.18 introduced dedicated CompiledVar and CompiledExists nodes
replacing BuiltinOperator { opcode: Var/Exists }. Update walk_node_for_vars to
extract variable paths from PathSegment slices so required_context_keys works
correctly and optimization tests pass.

Also replace the wasm_js getrandom feature (browser-only) with the unsupported
cfg backend via .cargo/config.toml. This eliminates __wbg_getRandomValues imports
that broke wasmtime, Chicory, and wazero runtimes. The evaluator never calls
getrandom at runtime since murmurhash3 is fully deterministic.

Regenerate flagd_evaluator.wasm with the clean WASM binary.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(evaluator): update CompiledNode variant matching for datalogic-rs fork

Switch to tuple-variant syntax for CustomOperator, StructuredObject,
CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex, CompiledThrow to match the refactored
struct layout in the patched datalogic-rs fork.

Use [patch.crates-io] with git URL pointing to fix/wasm-wasmbind-unconditional
branch in aepfli/datalogic-rs, which removes the unconditional chrono/wasmbind
dependency on wasm32 targets. This eliminates __wbg_* browser imports that break
wasmtime, Chicory, and wazero. PR upstream: GoPlasmatic/datalogic-rs#48.

Regenerate flagd_evaluator.wasm — binary is now clean of wasm-bindgen imports.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wasm): use datalogic-rs 4.0.19, no wasm-bindgen imports

Switch from git-patched fork to registry datalogic-rs 4.0.19 which fixed
the WASM browser dependency issue properly: the 'wasm' feature (chrono/wasmbind)
is now opt-in and disabled by default.

Use getrandom_backend="unsupported" cfg flag since nothing in the evaluation
path calls getrandom at runtime (ahash uses compile-time-rng, murmurhash3 is
deterministic). This produces a fully clean WASM binary with zero __wbg_*
imports.

Update walk_node_for_vars to use 4.0.19's tuple-variant CompiledNode API
(CustomOperator, StructuredObject, CompiledExists, CompiledSplitRegex,
CompiledThrow).

Result: flagd_evaluator.wasm has no wasm-bindgen imports at all — compatible
with Chicory, wazero, Wasmtime, and any other non-browser WASM runtime.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wasm): provide custom getrandom backend to prevent runtime panic

ahash's default features include runtime-rng, which calls getrandom at
startup to XOR a runtime-random value into the compile-time seed. With
getrandom_backend="unsupported", this panicked (unreachable trap) on
every update_state call across all language runtimes.

Switch to getrandom_backend="custom" and define __getrandom_v03_custom
to fill with zeros. This is safe: ahash already has a strong compile-time
seed via const-random, and the evaluator has no cryptographic randomness
requirements. Result: zero __wbg_* imports, no runtime panics.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The WASM binary no longer has __wbindgen_placeholder__ imports since

of any wasm-bindgen functions that are absent from the module.

The WASM module now has only one import: host::get_current_time_unix_seconds.
All wasm-bindgen imports (__wbindgen_placeholder__, __wbindgen_externref_xform__)
were eliminated by switching to getrandom_backend="custom" and datalogic-rs
without the wasm feature. Update docs and java/README.md accordingly.
